normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit precondition is required beyond having a mutable slice/array; the algorithm works for any length (including 0 and 1).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (i : Nat), i < arr.size → (i = 0 ∨ i > 0) → True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The outer loop iterates i from 1 to arr.len-1, so any access to arr[i] and arr[j-1] is within bounds when the loop conditions hold.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i : Nat), i ≤ arr.size →
  let arr' := (insertion_sort_prefix arr i) in
  Sorted (arr'.toList) ∧ Permutation arr.toList arr'.toList
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing indices up to i (i.e., after the outer loop has completed for all k < i), the prefix arr[0..i) is sorted in ascending order, and the multiset of elements in the whole array is unchanged (only swaps occur).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i j : Nat),
  i < arr.size → j ≤ i →
  let arr0 := arr in
  let arr1 := (insertion_sort_inner_step arr0 i j) in
  (Permutation arr0.toList arr1.toList) ∧
  (Sorted ( (arr1.toList).take i )) ∧
  (∀ k < j, arr1[k] ≤ arr1[k+1])
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: During the inner while-loop for a fixed i, with current index j, the elements outside the moving window are unchanged up to permutation, and the portion before j remains sorted; the swap step preserves the multiset and moves arr[j] left while maintaining sortedness of the already-processed prefix.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (i j : Nat), i < arr.size → j ≤ i →
  (j > 0 ∧ arr[j-1] > arr[j]) →
  (j-1) < j
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The inner loop decreases j by 1 each iteration, so it terminates.
The outer loop iterates over a finite range i = 1..arr.len-1, so it terminates.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int),
  let arr' := insertion_sort arr in
  Permutation arr.toList arr'.toList
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the implementation only uses swaps, the output array is a permutation of the input (same elements with the same multiplicities).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int),
  let arr' := insertion_sort arr in
  Sorted (arr'.toList)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After completion, the entire array is sorted in ascending order.
No stability property is required or claimed by the implementation; it performs swaps, so only sortedness and permutation are proven.
